# meta-toolkit

> A zero-dependency JavaScript toolkit for meta programming and OOP: name-casing conversions, property descriptor manipulation, aliases, prototype traversal, iterator helpers, deep path access, option merging, and comparator adapters.

- NPM: https://npmjs.org/package/meta-toolkit
- GitHub: https://github.com/uhop/meta-toolkit
- Wiki: https://github.com/uhop/meta-toolkit/wiki
- License: BSD-3-Clause
- Runtime: Node.js, Bun, Deno, browsers
- Module system: ESM (`"type": "module"`)
- TypeScript: hand-written `.d.ts` declarations for all modules

## Installation

```bash
npm install meta-toolkit
```

## Quick start

```js
import {fromKebabCase, toCamelCase, toPascalCase, toSnakeCase} from 'meta-toolkit/names.js';

const parts = fromKebabCase('foo-bar-baz');
console.log(toCamelCase(parts));  // 'fooBarBaz'
console.log(toPascalCase(parts)); // 'FooBarBaz'
console.log(toSnakeCase(parts));  // 'foo_bar_baz'
```

```js
import {addAliases} from 'meta-toolkit/aliases.js';

class Foo {
  constructor() { this.value = 2; }
  get double() { return this.value * 2; }
  line(a, b) { return a * this.value + b; }
}
addAliases(Foo.prototype, {
  double: 'x2, duplicate',
  line: 'linear'
});

const f = new Foo();
console.log(f.x2);         // 4
console.log(f.linear(1, 2)); // 4
```

```js
import {get, set, forceSet, remove} from 'meta-toolkit/path.js';

const obj = {};
forceSet(obj, 'a.b.c', 1); // obj = {a: {b: {c: 1}}}
get(obj, 'a.b.c');          // 1
set(obj, 'a.b.c', 2);      // obj = {a: {b: {c: 2}}}
remove(obj, 'a.b.c');       // obj = {a: {b: {}}}
```

---

## Module: Names (`meta-toolkit/names.js`)

Name-casing conversions. All `from*` functions split a string into `string[]`. All `to*` functions join `string[]` into a string.

```js
import {capitalize, fromCamelCase, toCamelCase, fromPascalCase, toPascalCase,
        fromSnakeCase, toSnakeCase, toAllCapsSnakeCase,
        fromKebabCase, toKebabCase} from 'meta-toolkit/names.js';
```

### Functions

- `capitalize(name: string): string` — capitalize first letter, lowercase rest.
- `fromCamelCase(name: string): string[]` — split camelCase into parts.
- `toCamelCase(names: string[]): string` — join parts as camelCase.
- `fromPascalCase(name: string): string[]` — split PascalCase into parts.
- `toPascalCase(names: string[]): string` — join parts as PascalCase.
- `fromSnakeCase(name: string): string[]` — split snake_case into parts.
- `toSnakeCase(names: string[]): string` — join parts as snake_case.
- `toAllCapsSnakeCase(names: string[]): string` — join parts as UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.
- `fromKebabCase(name: string): string[]` — split kebab-case into parts.
- `toKebabCase(names: string[]): string` — join parts as kebab-case.

### Example

```js
import {fromCamelCase, toKebabCase, toSnakeCase} from 'meta-toolkit/names.js';

const parts = fromCamelCase('fooBarBaz');
// parts = ['foo', 'Bar', 'Baz']

toKebabCase(parts);  // 'foo-bar-baz'
toSnakeCase(parts);  // 'foo-bar-baz' — note: parts are lowercased by toSnakeCase
```

---

## Module: Descriptors (`meta-toolkit/descriptors.js`)

Property descriptor creation and manipulation helpers.

```js
import {defaultDescriptor, makeGetter, makeSetter, makeAccessors,
        addDescriptor, addDescriptors, addAccessor, addGetters,
        copyDescriptors} from 'meta-toolkit/descriptors.js';
```

### Constants

- `defaultDescriptor` — `{configurable: true, enumerable: false}`. Used as the default `initDescriptor` for descriptor factories.

### Descriptor factories

- `makeGetter(getter: () => any, initDescriptor?: PropertyDescriptor): PropertyDescriptor` — create a getter descriptor.
- `makeSetter(setter: (value: any) => void, initDescriptor?: PropertyDescriptor): PropertyDescriptor` — create a setter descriptor.
- `makeAccessors(getter: () => any, setter: (value: any) => void, initDescriptor?: PropertyDescriptor): PropertyDescriptor` — create a getter+setter descriptor.

### Adding descriptors to objects

- `addDescriptor(target: object, names: string | symbol | (string|symbol)[], descriptor: PropertyDescriptor, force?: boolean): object` — define a descriptor on target under one or more names. `names` can be a comma-separated string. Skips existing properties unless `force` is truthy.
- `addDescriptors(target: object, dict: DescriptorDict, force?: boolean): object` — define multiple descriptors from a dictionary. Keys are comma-separated name strings or symbols; values are descriptors. Supports symbol keys.
- `addAccessor(target: object, names: string | symbol | (string|symbol)[], getter: () => any, setter: (value: any) => void, force?: boolean): object` — shorthand for `addDescriptor` + `makeAccessors`.
- `addGetters(target: object, dict: GetterDict, force?: boolean): object` — add multiple getters from a dictionary. Keys are comma-separated name strings; values are getter functions.

### Copying descriptors

- `copyDescriptors(target: object, source: object, names: string | symbol | (string|symbol)[] | AliasDict, force?: boolean): object` — copy descriptors from source to target. `names` can be:
  - A string (comma-separated) or symbol or array — copies those descriptors as-is.
  - An `AliasDict` object — keys are source property names, values are target alias names.

### Types

```ts
interface DescriptorDict {
  [name: string | symbol]: PropertyDescriptor;
}
interface GetterDict {
  [name: string | symbol]: () => any;
}
interface AliasDict {
  [name: string | symbol]: string | symbol | (string | symbol)[];
}
```

### Example

```js
import {makeGetter, addDescriptor, addGetters} from 'meta-toolkit/descriptors.js';

const obj = {_x: 10};
addDescriptor(obj, 'x, getX', makeGetter(function() { return this._x; }));
console.log(obj.x);    // 10
console.log(obj.getX);  // 10

addGetters(obj, {
  'doubled, x2': function() { return this._x * 2; }
});
console.log(obj.doubled); // 20
console.log(obj.x2);      // 20
```

---

## Module: Aliases (`meta-toolkit/aliases.js`)

Property aliasing built on `copyDescriptors` and `addDescriptor` from `descriptors.js`.

```js
import {addAlias, addAliases} from 'meta-toolkit/aliases.js';
```

### Functions

- `addAlias(object: object, name: string | symbol, aliases: string | symbol | (string|symbol)[], force?: boolean): object` — alias a single property. Copies the property descriptor from `name` to each alias.
- `addAliases(object: object, dict: string | symbol | (string|symbol)[] | AliasDict, force?: boolean): object` — alias multiple properties. Dict keys are source property names; values are alias names (comma-separated string, symbol, or array).

### Example

```js
import {addAlias, addAliases} from 'meta-toolkit/aliases.js';

class Queue {
  enqueue(value) { /* ... */ }
  dequeue() { /* ... */ }
}

addAlias(Queue.prototype, 'enqueue', 'push, add');
addAliases(Queue.prototype, {dequeue: 'pop, remove'});

// Now Queue instances have .push(), .add(), .pop(), .remove()
```

---

## Module: Iterators (`meta-toolkit/iterators.js`)

Iterator augmentation and lazy transformation helpers.

```js
import {augmentIterator, normalizeIterator, mapIterator, filterIterator} from 'meta-toolkit/iterators.js';
```

### Functions

- `augmentIterator<T>(iterator: Iterator<T>): IterableIterator<T>` — add `[Symbol.iterator]` returning `this` if not already present.
- `normalizeIterator<T>(iterator: Iterator<T>): IterableIterator<T>` — uses `Iterator.from()` if available (modern runtimes), otherwise falls back to `augmentIterator`.
- `mapIterator<T, R>(iterator: Iterable<T>, callbackFn: (value: T, index: number) => R): IterableIterator<R>` — lazy map. Uses native `iterator.map()` if available.
- `filterIterator<T>(iterator: Iterable<T>, callbackFn: (value: T, index: number) => boolean): IterableIterator<T>` — lazy filter. Uses native `iterator.filter()` if available.

### Example

```js
import {mapIterator, filterIterator} from 'meta-toolkit/iterators.js';

function* range(n) { for (let i = 0; i < n; ++i) yield i; }

const doubled = mapIterator(range(5), x => x * 2);
const evens = filterIterator(range(10), x => x % 2 === 0);

for (const v of doubled) console.log(v); // 0, 2, 4, 6, 8
for (const v of evens) console.log(v);   // 0, 2, 4, 6, 8
```

---

## Module: Prototypes (`meta-toolkit/prototypes.js`)

Prototype chain inspection utilities.

```js
import {prototypes, getPropertyDescriptor} from 'meta-toolkit/prototypes.js';
```

### Functions

- `prototypes(object: object, skipSelf?: boolean): Generator<object>` — generator yielding each prototype in the chain, stopping before `Object.prototype`. If `skipSelf` is truthy, skips the first (own) object.
- `getPropertyDescriptor(object: object, name: string | symbol): PropertyDescriptor | undefined` — like `Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor` but walks the entire prototype chain.

### Example

```js
import {prototypes, getPropertyDescriptor} from 'meta-toolkit/prototypes.js';

class A { foo() {} }
class B extends A { bar() {} }

for (const proto of prototypes(new B())) {
  console.log(Object.getOwnPropertyNames(proto));
}
// ['constructor', 'bar']
// ['constructor', 'foo']

const desc = getPropertyDescriptor(new B(), 'foo');
console.log(typeof desc.value); // 'function'
```

---

## Module: Path (`meta-toolkit/path.js`)

Deep property access by dot-delimited string path or array of keys.

```js
import {get, set, forceSet, remove} from 'meta-toolkit/path.js';
```

### PathOptions

```ts
interface PathOptions {
  delimiter?: string | RegExp;  // Default: '.'
  defaultValue?: any;
}
```

### Functions

- `get(object: object, path: string | (string|symbol)[], options?: PathOptions): any` — get a nested value. Returns `options.defaultValue` if the path does not exist.
- `set(object: object, path: string | (string|symbol)[], value: any, options?: PathOptions): any` — set a nested value. The path must already exist (intermediate objects are not created). Returns the previous value, or `options.defaultValue` if the path did not exist.
- `forceSet(object: object, path: string | (string|symbol)[], value: any, options?: PathOptions): any` — set a nested value, creating intermediate empty objects `{}` as needed. Throws `TypeError` if the root object is not dereferenceable or the path is empty.
- `remove(object: object, path: string | (string|symbol)[], options?: PathOptions): any` — delete a nested property. Returns the removed value, or `options.defaultValue` if the path did not exist.

### Example

```js
import {get, set, forceSet, remove} from 'meta-toolkit/path.js';

const config = {};
forceSet(config, 'db.host', 'localhost');
forceSet(config, 'db.port', 5432);
// config = {db: {host: 'localhost', port: 5432}}

get(config, 'db.host');              // 'localhost'
get(config, 'db.user', {defaultValue: 'root'}); // 'root'

set(config, 'db.port', 3306);
remove(config, 'db.host');
// config = {db: {port: 3306}}
```

---

## Module: Options (`meta-toolkit/options.js`)

Constructor option merging utility.

```js
import {copyOptions} from 'meta-toolkit/options.js';
```

### Functions

- `copyOptions(target: object, defaults: object, ...sources: object[]): object` — merge defaults then sources into target. If `target` is null/undefined, a new object is created. First, all non-undefined values from `defaults` are copied to `target`. Then, for each source, only keys that exist in `defaults` are copied. Returns `target`.

### Example

```js
import {copyOptions} from 'meta-toolkit/options.js';

const defaults = {color: 'red', size: 10, visible: true};
const userOpts = {size: 20, extra: 'ignored'};

const result = copyOptions({}, defaults, userOpts);
// result = {color: 'red', size: 20, visible: true}
// 'extra' is ignored because it's not in defaults
```

---

## Module: Comparators (`meta-toolkit/comparators.js`)

Adapters between `less` (`(a, b) => boolean`), `compare` (`(a, b) => number`), and `equal` function styles.

```js
import {compareFromLess, lessFromCompare, equalFromLess,
        reverseLess, reverseCompare} from 'meta-toolkit/comparators.js';
```

### Functions

- `compareFromLess(lessFn: (a, b) => boolean): (a, b) => number` — adapt a less function to a compare function. Returns -1, 0, or 1.
- `lessFromCompare(compareFn: (a, b) => number): (a, b) => boolean` — adapt a compare function to a less function.
- `equalFromLess(lessFn: (a, b) => boolean): (a, b) => boolean` — derive equality: returns true if neither `less(a,b)` nor `less(b,a)`.
- `reverseLess(lessFn: (a, b) => boolean): (a, b) => boolean` — reverse a less function (swaps arguments).
- `reverseCompare(compareFn: (a, b) => number): (a, b) => number` — reverse a compare function (swaps arguments).

### Example

```js
import {compareFromLess, reverseLess} from 'meta-toolkit/comparators.js';

const less = (a, b) => a < b;
const compare = compareFromLess(less);

console.log(compare(1, 2));  // -1
console.log(compare(2, 1));  //  1
console.log(compare(1, 1));  //  0

const descLess = reverseLess(less);
console.log(descLess(1, 2)); // false
console.log(descLess(2, 1)); // true

[3, 1, 2].sort(compare);                        // [1, 2, 3]
[3, 1, 2].sort(compareFromLess(descLess));       // [3, 2, 1]
```

---

## Common patterns

### Name conversion pipeline

```js
import {fromCamelCase, toKebabCase, toPascalCase, toSnakeCase} from 'meta-toolkit/names.js';

const parts = fromCamelCase('myVariableName');
console.log(toKebabCase(parts));   // 'my-variable-name'
console.log(toPascalCase(parts));  // 'MyVariableName'
console.log(toSnakeCase(parts));   // 'my_variable_name'
```

### Dynamic accessor generation

```js
import {addAccessor} from 'meta-toolkit/descriptors.js';

class Point {
  constructor(x, y) { this._x = x; this._y = y; }
}

addAccessor(Point.prototype, 'x',
  function() { return this._x; },
  function(v) { this._x = v; }
);

const p = new Point(1, 2);
console.log(p.x); // 1
p.x = 10;
console.log(p.x); // 10
```

### Aliasing methods for API compatibility

```js
import {addAliases} from 'meta-toolkit/aliases.js';

class Collection {
  pushBack(value) { /* ... */ }
  popFront() { /* ... */ }
  getLength() { /* ... */ }
}

addAliases(Collection.prototype, {
  pushBack: 'push, add, enqueue',
  popFront: 'pop, remove, dequeue',
  getLength: 'size, length'
});
```

### Lazy iterator chaining

```js
import {mapIterator, filterIterator} from 'meta-toolkit/iterators.js';

function* integers() { for (let i = 0; ; ++i) yield i; }

// Lazy: nothing computed until iterated
const pipeline = mapIterator(
  filterIterator(integers(), n => n % 3 === 0),
  n => n * n
);

// Take first 5
let count = 0;
for (const v of pipeline) {
  console.log(v); // 0, 9, 36, 81, 144
  if (++count >= 5) break;
}
```

### Deep configuration management

```js
import {get, forceSet, remove} from 'meta-toolkit/path.js';

const config = {};
forceSet(config, 'server.host', '0.0.0.0');
forceSet(config, 'server.port', 8080);
forceSet(config, 'db.connection.host', 'localhost');
forceSet(config, 'db.connection.port', 5432);

console.log(get(config, 'server'));
// {host: '0.0.0.0', port: 8080}

remove(config, 'db.connection.port');
console.log(get(config, 'db.connection'));
// {host: 'localhost'}
```
